title: nelumbo nucifera var. lutea
intro: Nelumbo nucifera var. lutea, also known as the yellow lotus, is a rare and vibrant variety of the Nelumbo nucifera plant, which is a type of aquatic plant native to Asia and known for its beautiful flowers and edible rhizomes. The yellow lotus is believed to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and alkaloids. The rhizomes of the yellow lotus are said to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving digestive issues, and can be used to treat various ailments, such as diarrhea, dysentery, and skin conditions. In traditional Chinese medicine, the yellow lotus is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or decoctions to harness its medicinal properties, and its leaves and flowers are also used to make herbal preparations, such as poultices and salves.
